commands.review_summary:
Filters replay-unsafe sessions_send branch entries from compaction-safeguard fallback history and adds focused regression tests for source- and target-session branch shapes.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +148. Total +255 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. not as a high-confidence live current-main workflow. Source inspection matches the linked report's fallback log path, and the PR adds replay-shaped regression and terminal proof coverage for that branch path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the stateful fallback/message-delivery risk and whether terminal production-hook proof is enough.

target.title: fix(compaction): skip replay-unsafe session sends
target.body:
Fixes #84139.

## Summary

- Filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` branch entries before the compaction safeguard uses session-branch fallback history.
- Keeps the existing visible-branch fallback for ordinary custom/user/tool history so empty-preparation compaction still writes a boundary instead of re-entering the empty compaction loop.
- Adds focused regressions for both sides of the duplicate path: the target session's inter-session input turn and the source session's already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source invariant for the compaction safeguard branch fallback is that it may recover real conversation content only when the recovered branch entries are safe to summarize as prior history. Before this patch, `collectSessionBranchMessages()` fed the fallback path every branch entry because empty compaction preparation had lost the normal message set; that treated side-effecting `sessions_send` delivery artifacts as ordinary conversation and let compaction recovery reintroduce already-executed inter-session send turns.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix is at the branch-fallback source boundary, before `containsRealConversation()` and summarization decide whether recovered branch content is usable. It removes only replay-unsafe messaging send artifacts from that recovered branch: target-session `inter_session` user inputs whose source tool is `sessions_send`, their immediate assistant/tool reply, and source-session messaging send tool calls/results. Ordinary visible custom branch entries, normal user/assistant history, and non-messaging tool results continue through the existing fallback path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The added fallback wording is scoped to the existing compaction safeguard branch fallback path, not a new runtime fallback. The filter preserves the prior empty-boundary guard for no-real-content compaction and avoids broad catch/default behavior changes.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth contract for replay-unsafe messaging send detection remains `isMessagingToolSendAction()`. The compaction safeguard now reuses that same contract instead of special-casing only transcript text, while `inputProvenance` remains the canonical marker for delivered inter-session user inputs.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** No config, schema, API, migration, or default compaction settings changed. Existing ordinary branch fallback behavior remains covered by the existing test that preserves visible custom branch entries and read tool results.
- **Related open PR scan:** The recorded related open PR scan reported `related_open_prs: []` and `scan_error: null`, so this is not competing with a broader same-contract PR found by the daily-fix scan.
- **Out of scope:** External gateway UI/Mantis/Crabbox capture, unrelated stale messaging-tool dedupe state, provider summarizer behavior, session storage format, and non-`sessions_send` product semantics are intentionally not changed.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:stateful-runtime`, `merge-risk:message-delivery`, and external live-proof gap risk were considered because the bug is in compaction/session recovery behavior and the exact external UI/Mantis run was not executed.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes only which recovered branch entries are allowed into compaction fallback summarization after compaction preparation omitted real content. It does not change delivery, idempotency keys, session storage, or the normal compaction preparation path.
- **Why acceptable:** The focused regressions exercise the exact branch-fallback seam for both source and target `sessions_send` artifacts, the acceptance suite covers compaction overflow plus sessions_send/A2A behavior, and the after-fix terminal proof below runs the production compaction safeguard hook path against replay-shaped source/target session branches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Prevents compaction safeguard branch fallback from reusing already-executed `sessions_send` interactions as fallback history during compaction recovery, while preserving ordinary visible branch history fallback.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at `/media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293`. The terminal proof was an evidence-only script run with `pnpm exec tsx`; it did not use Vitest and imported the current PR's production `compactionSafeguardExtension`, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 pnpm exec tsx /media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93293-evidence/proof-84139-sessions-send-compaction-replay.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[proof-84139] Real-runtime terminal proof for PR #93293 / issue #84139.
[proof-84139] Production paths: compactionSafeguardExtension session_before_compact, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: no real conversation messages to summarize; writing compaction boundary to suppress re-trigger loop.

=== Target session after sessions_send delivery ===
branch reply count before compaction: 1
compaction provider calls for target branch: 0
provider-visible target reply count: 0
summary wrote empty boundary: true
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: using session branch messages after compaction preparation omitted real conversation content.

=== Source session after sessions_send tool execution ===
provider message roles: ["user"]
provider sessions_send artifact count: 0
provider preserved requester ask: true

[proof-84139] All exactly-once replay-safety assertions passed.
```

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  194 passed (194)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 33.76s
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The terminal proof starts with one already-delivered target-session reply in the session branch and shows the compaction provider sees zero target-branch messages and zero copies of that reply, so compaction recovery does not replay the delivered `sessions_send` turn. It also shows the source-session fallback provider receives only the original requester user message, with `provider sessions_send artifact count: 0`, so the already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result is not reintroduced as fallback history.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run an external gateway UI, Mantis, Crabbox, or third-party channel/provider live capture. The added proof is local terminal/log proof over the production compaction safeguard hook path and production replay-safety contracts, not an external visual recording.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — fixed:** Added terminal/log proof showing the replay-shaped target session starts with exactly one already-delivered `sessions_send` reply, while the production compaction safeguard hook path sends zero target-branch messages and zero copies of that reply to the compaction provider. External gateway UI, Mantis, and Crabbox proof remain explicitly listed as not tested.
- **RF-002 — fixed:** Replaced the prior Vitest-only evidence with a non-Vitest `pnpm exec tsx` terminal/log proof that imports the PR worktree's production `compactionSafeguardExtension`, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection. The body still discloses the unavailable external live environment.
- **RF-003 — fixed:** The after-fix proof is no longer only local Vitest output; the evidence now includes the production-hook terminal proof plus the focused acceptance test shard and `git diff --check`.
- **RF-004 — fixed:** The risk section explains the fallback boundary and why the filter is scoped to replay-unsafe `sessions_send` artifacts only. The proof covers both over-filtering-sensitive source history preservation (`provider message roles: ["user"]`) and duplicate-delivery prevention on the target branch (`provider-visible target reply count: 0`).
- **RF-005 — maintainer-decision:** No additional code defect was found in the current patch. If maintainers require external gateway UI/Mantis/Crabbox proof beyond the supplied terminal/log production-hook proof, that remaining requirement needs maintainer-provided environment proof or proof override.

## Regression coverage

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Empty compaction preparation with session branch fallback now filters target-side `inter_session` inputs from `sessions_send` and source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result entries.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression sits at the branch-fallback seam that produced the duplicate recovery behavior, without requiring a full multi-agent live run or changing sessions_send delivery code.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
